issues: Time line inconsistency: Google announced Transformer migration in 2016 (blog), but paper was published in 2017; no clarification provided.
Unverified claim: 'training time shortened by 40%' lacks specific task reference (WMT 2016 vs. other benchmarks).
suggestions: Clarify: 'Google announced Transformer migration for Google Translate in 2016 (blog), with the paper published in 2017.'
Specify: 'In WMT 2016 English-German translation, Transformer reduced training time by 40% compared to LSTM-based seq2seq.'

issues: Temporal inconsistency: GNMT (2016) is incorrectly placed under 'seq2seq architecture (2014)' without distinguishing architecture emergence vs. model implementation.
suggestions: Revise to: 'Seq2seq architecture emerged in 2014; Google's GNMT model (2016) implemented it using dual LSTMs.'

issues: Critical omission: No mathematical derivation for self-attention mechanism (e.g., Q/K/V computation, softmax formula).
Technical error: 'training time independent of sequence length' contradicts O(n²) complexity (should state quadratic scaling).
Unverified performance claim: '10x faster' lacks citation to paper's experimental data.
suggestions: Add core formulas: 'Self-attention computes attention scores as Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k)) V, where Q=XW_Q, K=XW_K, V=XW_V.'
Correct statement: 'Training time scales quadratically with sequence length (O(n²)), but parallelization mitigates practical overhead.'
Cite paper: 'Per Table 1 in Vaswani et al. (2017), Transformer achieved 10x speedup over LSTM on 512-token sequences (V100 GPU).'

issues: BLEU score (28.4) not contextualized (e.g., no comparison to prior models or task details).
suggestions: Specify: 'Achieved 28.4 BLEU on WMT 2016 English-German translation, surpassing previous seq2seq models by 3.2 BLEU points.'
